column1: # 1. SDF eval: frep4 (cpu_ir-simd8) and libfive (cpu-array) are the same class

column2: 60-120x over the hyperfun interpreter. libfive edges ahead

column1: #    on simple CSG; frep4 leads on twist (SIMD hides the trig).
# 2. mpr (CUDA rasteriser) is fast on polynomial CSG (s1-s3: 8-40 ms) but
#    CATASTROPHIC on trig-heavy fields: gyroid @1024 = 19485 ms (19.5 s!)
#    twist @512 = 114 ms. Energy tracks it — gyroid/twist burn ~250x the CSG
#    scenes. Root cause: MPR's interval/affine tape reduction can't prune sin/cos
#    fields (intervals explode). frep4 and libfive don't rely on tape reduction
#    so they stay flat on the same scenes. This is a fundamental MPR property
#    a tuning issue — a clean differentiator for the paper.
# 3. libfive grid on imported many-primitive scenes (@193) is very slow
#    (prospero 252 s) — its array evaluator doesn't prune wide CSG the way its
#    own interval renderer would; frep4 cpu_ir-simd8 stays ~1 s on the same.
# Caveats: frep4 render is iGPU (no RT cores -> gpu_rtx runs software BVH
# doesn't beat gpu_glsl here; the discrete RTX 2080 will flip that). gpu_ir needs
# SPIRV-LLVM-Translator. mpr does render3d only (it's a GPU rasteriser
